g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the prof fill in when creating an assignment?')
g.text: title, description, rubric, difficulty, deadline, optional reference docs (PDFs)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Defendly send calendar invites to students?')
g.text: invite + unique token URL per student

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is stored in Firestore when an assignment is created?')
g.text: assignment doc, tokens, ref doc files

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What happens when the student opens their unique link?')
g.text: Student opens calendar link → unique token URL
sees assignment details, rubric, deadline — uploads submission (PDF / doc)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What metadata is saved to Firestore on submission?')

g.text: reads: student submission + assignment brief + rubric + ref docs → extracts concepts, claims, weak areas, rubric gaps
generates 3–5 questions: specific to submission AND constrained by rubric + scope

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Firestore store after agents run?')
sendPrompt('How does the live voice defense session work in the browser?')
g.text: Cloud TTS speaks questions → student answers via mic → Cloud STT transcribes → agent probes vague answers

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Where is the defense recording stored?')
sendPrompt('What does the comprehension report contain?')
g.text: score, understands / weak in / cannot justify, per-rubric verdict, recommendation

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the student see on the score screen?')
sendPrompt('What does the professor see in the dashboard?')
